items.title: 'All eyes are on the performance' as Canberra to Bali flights take off again

children.content: Virgin Australia launches direct flights from Canberra to Bali, becoming the first Australian carrier to fly internationally out of the capital.

items.title: 'Left an enormous hole': Man sentenced after crash killed Canberra father

children.content: A Canberra man has been spared jail after being found guilty of negligent driving causing the death of motorcyclist and father Craig O'Neill in a crash two years ago.

image.altText: A crumpled motorbike on the ground near a parked truck, at night at an intersection.

items.title: 'Took my breath away': Afghanistan gallery opening soon at Australian War Memorial

children.content: Australian War Memorial director Matt Anderson is asked about an invitation issued to Ben Roberts-Smith, who has been accused of war crimes, to the upcoming opening of a new gallery space dedicated to Australia's service in Afghanistan.

image.altText: A painting of a man lying down, with strong colours and brushstrokes.

items.title: Seeking equality, Afghan cricketers head to world cup they cannot play in

children.content: In a bid for recognition, the Afghanistan women's cricket team will travel to England for the T20 World Cup — an event they cannot play in.

items.title: New country choir boosts queer visibility on NSW South Coast

children.content: A choir formed in the tiny village of Candelo, on the New South Wales Far South Coast, is giving a voice to the LGBTQ+ community.

image.altText: a group of people singing and smiling

items.title: This seaweed is the ocean's lungs. Can changing its genes save it?

children.content: Scientists are investigating whether gene editing could become a tool for helping kelp adapt to the increasing challenges posed by climate change.

image.altText: bull kelp under water

items.title: 'We lost our son': India's medical exams scandal leads to tragedy and uproar

children.content: More than 2 million students across India have resat the country's highly competitive medical entrance exam after last month's test was cancelled amid allegations that questions had been leaked.

items.title: Chihuahua Jet takes to the air as more pets allowed in cabin

items.title: 'They've been caught out': KPMG fallout continues with resignations

children.content: Alessandro Circati's decision to play for the Socceroos raised eyebrows within his family in Italy, but his father says they now realise that, deep down, he's not Italian.

items.title: Can Australia still make the knockout rounds of the World Cup?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is well underway and it's starting to become clear which teams are progressing through to the next round of competition. But, Australia's fate still isn't sealed.
Australia currently sitting second in its group with one win against Türkiye and one loss against the United States.
We break down how the new qualifying process works, what's changed, and what the Socceroos need from their match on Friday against Paraguay.
